name: zero_len_write_changes_view
code:
proof fn phi_1_zero_len_write_changes_view(
    view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    addr: int,
)
    requires
        0 <= addr,
        addr <= view.len(),
    ensures
        view.write(addr, Seq::<u8>::empty()) != view,
{
}
reason: Writing zero bytes should be a no-op; if the spec entails a state change, the write postcondition is too broad

name: write_changes_view_length
code:
proof fn phi_2_write_changes_view_length(
    view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    addr: int,
    bytes: Seq<u8>,
)
    requires
        0 <= addr,
        addr + bytes.len() <= view.len(),
        bytes.len() > 0,
    ensures
        view.write(addr, bytes).len() != view.len(),
{
}
reason: A write should never change the length of the subregion view; if it can, the ensures of write_absolute fails to preserve region geometry

name: write_modifies_outside_range
code:
proof fn phi_3_write_modifies_outside_range(
    view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    addr: int,
    bytes: Seq<u8>,
    k: int,
)
    requires
        0 <= addr,
        addr + bytes.len() <= view.len(),
        bytes.len() > 0,
        0 <= k < view.len(),
        !(addr <= k < addr + bytes.len()),
    ensures
        view.write(addr, bytes).state[k] != view.state[k],
{
}
reason: Bytes outside the written range must be unchanged; if the spec allows modification of untouched positions, data corruption is possible

name: write_not_idempotent
code:
proof fn phi_4_write_not_idempotent(
    view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    addr: int,
    bytes: Seq<u8>,
)
    requires
        0 <= addr,
        addr + bytes.len() <= view.len(),
        bytes.len() > 0,
    ensures
        view.write(addr, bytes).write(addr, bytes) != view.write(addr, bytes),
{
}
reason: Writing the same bytes to the same location twice should be idempotent; non-idempotency would indicate the write spec accumulates phantom state

name: write_creates_outstanding_outside_range
code:
proof fn phi_5_write_creates_outstanding_outside_range(
    view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    addr: int,
    bytes: Seq<u8>,
    k: int,
)
    requires
        view.no_outstanding_writes_in_range(0, view.len() as int),
        0 <= addr,
        addr + bytes.len() <= view.len(),
        bytes.len() > 0,
        0 <= k < view.len(),
        !(addr <= k < addr + bytes.len()),
    ensures
        view.write(addr, bytes).state[k].outstanding_write.is_some(),
{
}
reason: A write must not create outstanding writes at positions outside the target range; if it can, flush semantics for unrelated regions become unsound
